overview: Jeskai Control is an Azorius Control variant that splashes red to effectively handle the creature-heavy Standard meta. The deck aims to control the board using spot removal, counterspells, and lifegain until the opponent exhausts their resources. Once the board is stabilized, it transitions to powerful win conditions to close out the game.

keep_criteria: Versatile hands that perform well across multiple matchups
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetch/shock sources), a turn-1 discard spell, and a turn-2 interaction (counterspell, removal, or bounce).
Prioritize keeping lands that can fetch basics for Stock Up and Jeskai Revelation conditions.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or land drops
Risky hands against unknown opponents that rely on topdeck manipulation
Mulligan to 5+ lands, pure removal without answers, or hands with multiple draw spells but no early interaction.

examples.reason: Has ways to interact with the opponent but needs land drops to put plans in motion.
Can perform really well in many matchups and is versatile enough to keep in most cases.

early_game: Use spot removal and counterspells to control the board
Gain life to survive aggro strategies
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize or Inquisition on the opponent's most threatening early card or key combo piece.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a counterspell or removal on the opponent's second-drop threat.
If ahead, use Stock Up or Consult the Star Charts to dig for answers.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast colored spells on curve while maintaining land drops.
mid_game: Continue controlling the board and preventing opponent value
Bounce problematic creatures with Marang River Regent
Manage resources and keep backup plans in hand
Preserve mana for multiple interaction spells per turn
never overcommit to a single threat if you lack answers.
Use Consult the Star Charts to find specific answers or finishers rather than randomizing.
Manage graveyard by using Rest in Peace or Leyline of the Void if needed, and avoid milling yourself with Jeskai Revelation unless you have a win condition or can discard non-essential cards.
Hold back on committing your best threat until you have at least one interaction spell in hand.
Trade efficiently: use removal on key threats, counterspells on game-ending spells, and discard on combo pieces.
late_game: Deploy win conditions like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Beza, the Bounding Spring
Finish the game once the opponent runs out of resources

Teferi, Hero of Dominaria (planeswalker loyalty plays), Jeska's Thane (attacking with creatures), The Wandering Emperor (combat tricks + card draw), or a well-timed Jeskai Revelation into a lethal threat.
Secure the game by establishing a board presence that can't be easily wiped, then close with combat or a finisher.
Go all-in when you have card advantage locked, the opponent has no removal/counterspells in hand, or you've established a board that can trade favorably.
Commit when you can safely cast Jeskai Revelation and still have a win condition in hand or on board.

key_cards.name: Elspeth, Storm Slayer
key_cards.notes: Fills board with tokens, buffs creatures with evasion, and removes 3+ cost creatures.

key_cards.name: Beza, the Bounding Spring
key_cards.notes: Deals with aggro decks, interacts well with Elspeth, and finishes games.

key_cards.name: Marang River Regent
key_cards.notes: Excellent in combat, creates card advantage, and bounces creatures.

key_cards.name: Ride's End
key_cards.notes: Deals with recursive creatures like Mosswood Dreadknight.

key_cards.name: Tishana's Tidebinder
key_cards.notes: Versatile ability lets you block many meta cards.

key_cards.name: Regal Caracal
key_cards.notes: One of the best cards against aggro, complements Beza.

sequencing_tips: Don't pass turns without mana open against bounce strategies.
Keep global removals in hand for the right timing.
Control your opponent's board and life points carefully against aggro.
Prevent opponents from creating value with creatures, especially when Cauldron is in play.
Use removals wisely and save them for the right time.
Jeskai Revelation is a double-edged sword
only cast it when you have a clear win condition or can discard non-essential cards.
Consult the Star Charts is best used to find specific answers or finishers, not as a random draw spell.
Jeskai's color fixing relies on fetch lands and shock lands, so they may be vulnerable to early pressure.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without answers, as Jeskai Control is vulnerable to sweepers.

matchups.advice: Control the opponent and prevent them from creating value with creatures, especially if Cauldron is in play.
Control the board and prevent value from problematic cards; Jeskai has the upper hand in game 1.
Play around the opponent's sequences and avoid passing turns without mana.
Control the board and life points carefully, using removals at the right time.
Disrupt the combo and game plan in any way possible until you can deploy your win conditions.
watch_for: damage when artifacts leave the board

matchups.advice: Use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Cast threats at end of opponent's turn or in response to their spells. Use Consult the Star Charts to find uncounterable threats or protection. Employ cheap, efficient threats that can trade with their interaction.

Prioritize early interaction to disrupt mana acceleration. Use Thoughtseize/Inquisition on ramp spells. Bring in artifact/enchantment hate and board wipes. If they get ahead, use Teferi or Karn to stabilize and lock them out.

Keep early interaction (counters, removal, discard). Play aggressively with your own threats once stabilized. Use Swan Song or Dovin's Veto to protect your board. Don't overextend; trade efficiently and close the game quickly once you stabilize.

Out-value them with superior card draw (Stock Up, Consult the Star Charts, Jeskai Revelation). Apply pressure with resilient threats they can't easily remove. Force them to use interaction on non-critical spells. Win the attrition war by having more answers and better late-game threats.

Win the discard war. Prioritize keeping hands with multiple interaction spells. Use Thoughtseize/Inquisition to strip their answers and force them to mulligan. Trade efficiently, save your best threat for when they're tapped out. Use Consult the Star Charts to find the exact answer they need.

common_threats: Watch for opponent's mana base (dual lands vs. shock lands vs. fetches) to predict their interaction.
Track graveyard content for reanimation or storm threats.
Note when they tap out for a big spell
that's your window to attack or cast a free interaction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Jeskai Control is an Azorius Control variant that splashes to red to take advantage of how valuable this color is in many matchups. ... The main game plan is simple: all you have to do is control the game until your opponent runs out of resources and you can easily play your win condition.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Elspeth, Storm Slayer is that win condition.
It is also a great finisher.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: don't pass turns without any mana because this could be fatal.
Keep a few global removals for the right time, and use them wisely.
don't give your opponent even an inch of freedom, and always have a backup plan in hand.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Vivi Ornitier is still dominating Magic: The Gathering.
Be careful with Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and his Ninjutsu.
Be careful with Screaming Nemesis because it can be very problematic for you
Ride's End deals with recursive creatures (like Mosswood Dreadknight) really well.
Basically, your opponent will use Weapons Manufacturing to fill their board with artifacts.
